title: Apporximate Shadow Volume for smooth geometry
author: M. S

body.content: “Shadow volume” is a 3D graphics technique to compute crisp, high-fidelity shadows. An important bottleneck to the technique is the high CPU load necessary to extract the shadow geometry. When the shadow caster is sufficiently smooth, however, this geometry extraction becomes trivial—and the shadowing artifacts are kept to a minimum. Here is an illustration with some smooth meshes (and a skinned character)!
